part.text:
# Interactive Diff Editing

## Background
You have a jj repository at `/home/user/myproject`. There is a commit with description `add features` that added two functions `foo()` and `bar()` in `app.py`. You want to remove the `bar()` function from that commit, leaving only `foo()` in it.

## Requirements
- The commit with description `add features` must be modified to only contain the `foo()` function.
- The `bar()` function must be completely removed from the commit's changes.
- The working copy must remain clean (no modifications in `@`).

## Implementation
1. Navigate to `/home/user/myproject`.
2. Modify the commit. You can use `jj diffedit` with a custom `--tool` script, or use `jj edit` along with file modifications and `jj commit`/`jj squash` to achieve the desired state.

## Constraints
- Project path: `/home/user/myproject`

Current Working Directory: /
Current Time: Wed Apr 22 2026 04:51:45 GMT+0000 (Coordinated Universal Time)

# GIT STATUS

# TODOs

# Interactive Diff Editing

## Background
You have a jj repository at `/home/user/myproject`. There is a commit with description `add features` that added two functions `foo()` and `bar()` in `app.py`. You want to remove the `bar()` function from that commit, leaving only `foo()` in it.

## Requirements
- The commit with description `add features` must be modified to only contain the `foo()` function.
- The `bar()` function must be completely removed from the commit's changes.
- The working copy must remain clean (no modifications in `@`).

## Implementation
1. Navigate to `/home/user/myproject`.
2. Modify the commit. You can use `jj diffedit` with a custom `--tool` script, or use `jj edit` along with file modifications and `jj commit`/`jj squash` to achieve the desired state.

## Constraints
- Project path: `/home/user/myproject`

input.command: cd /home/user/myproject && ls -la && jj log
cd /home/user/myproject && ls -la && jj log
cd /home/user/myproject && ls -la && jj log

output.output:
total 4
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 14 Apr 14 17:31 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 18 Apr 14 17:31 ..
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 48 Apr 14 17:31 .git
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 32 Apr 14 17:31 .jj
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 57 Apr 14 17:31 app.py
@  wzooszsr your@email.com 2026-04-14 17:31:50 1b4f6d18
│  (empty) (no description set)
○  yskwmtpw your@email.com 2026-04-14 17:31:50 5e601526
│  add features
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

input.command: cd /home/user/myproject && cat app.py && echo "---" && jj
cd /home/user/myproject && cat app.py && echo "---" && jj diff -r yskwmtpw
cd /home/user/myproject && cat app.py && echo "---" && jj diff -r yskwmtpw
cd /home/user/myproject && cat app.py && echo "---" && jj diff -r yskwmtpw

output.output:
def foo():
    print("foo")

def bar():
    print("bar")
---
Added regular file app.py:
        1: def foo():
        2:     print("foo")
        3: 
        4: def bar():
        5:     print("bar")

input.command: cd /home/user/myproject && jj edit yskwmtpw
cd /home/user/myproject && jj edit yskwmtpw
cd /home/user/myproject && jj edit yskwmtpw

output.output:
Working copy  (@) now at: yskwmtpw 5e601526 add features
Parent commit (@-)      : zzzzzzzz 00000000 (empty) (no description set)

output.error: File has not been read yet. Please read the file before writing it.

cd /home/user/myproject && printf 'def foo():\n    print("foo")\n' > app.py && cat app.py
cd /home/user/myproject && printf 'def foo():\n    print("foo")\n' > app.py && cat app.py
cd /home/user/myproject && printf 'def foo():\n    print("foo")\n' > app.py && cat app.py
cd /home/user/myproject && jj edit wzooszsr && jj log
cd /home/user/myproject && jj edit wzooszsr && jj log && echo "---" && cat app.py && echo "---" && jj diff -r yskwmtpw && echo "---" && jj
cd /home/user/myproject && jj edit wzooszsr && jj log && echo "---" && cat app.py && echo "---" && jj diff -r yskwmtpw && echo "---" && jj status
cd /home/user/myproject && jj edit wzooszsr && jj log && echo "---" && cat app.py && echo "---" && jj diff -r yskwmtpw && echo "---" && jj status
cd /home/user/myproject && jj edit wzooszsr && jj log && echo "---" && cat app.py && echo "---" && jj diff -r yskwmtpw && echo "---" && jj status
cd /home/user/myproject && jj log
cd /home/user/myproject && jj log
cd /home/user/myproject && jj log

output.output:
@  yskwmtpw your@email.com 2026-04-22 04:52:29 f5a91be5
│  add features
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

input.command: cd /home/user/myproject && jj new && jj log && echo "---" && cat app.py && echo "---" && jj diff -
cd /home/user/myproject && jj new && jj log && echo "---" && cat app.py && echo "---" && jj diff -r 'description("add features")' && echo "---STATUS---" && jj status
cd /home/user/myproject && jj new && jj log && echo "---" && cat app.py && echo "---" && jj diff -r 'description("add features")' && echo "---STATUS---" && jj status
cd /home/user/myproject && jj new && jj log && echo "---" && cat app.py && echo "---" && jj diff -r 'description("add features")' && echo "---STATUS---" && jj status

@  trywnysq your@email.com 2026-04-22 04:52:47 4468d6b3
│  (empty) (no description set)
○  yskwmtpw your@email.com 2026-04-22 04:52:29 f5a91be5
│  add features
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000
---

    print("foo")
---

Working copy  (@) now at: trywnysq 4468d6b3 (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : yskwmtpw f5a91be5 add features

part.text: The commit has been successfully modified.
